rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 22, 2009, LINE 5, PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB'S Motion For Reconsideration. MOTION DENIED. JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL WAS SIGNED BY JUDGE MAHONEY AND FILED FEBRUARY 13, 2009. THE COURT LOSES JURISDICTION TO RULE ON A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WHEN JUDGMENT IS ENTERED. SEE APRI INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1999) 76 CAL.APP.4TH 176, 181. PRIOR RECONSIDERATION MOTION WAS DENIED, AND NO NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW ARE RAISED BY CURRENT RECONSIDERATION MOTION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST VS. THE ESTATE OF YIU SUN NG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Third Amended Cross-Complaint As To The 4th And 6th Causes Of Action; Pos; P/A, Declaration Filed
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 22, 2009, LINE 7, CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG'S Demurrer To Third Amended Cross-Complaint As To The 4th And 6th Causes Of Action. DEMURRER OVERRULED. THE COURT CANNOT RULE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT CLEARLY DISCLOSE THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED AND THE DISCOVERY RULE MAY APPLY IN THIS CASE. FURTHER, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS NOT AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFF AS TO DEFENDANTS' COUNTERCLAIM IF THE PERIOD HAS NOT RUN ON IT AT THE TIME OF THE COMMENCEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION EVEN THOUGH IT HAS RUN WHEN THE COUNTERCLAIM IS PLEADED. JONES V. MORTIMER (1946) 28 CAL. 2ND 627, 633. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. LUXOR CAB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 22, 2009, LINE 8, PLAINTIFF PATRICK HELLAND'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. MOTION GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S 10TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE SHALL READ "PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY OPERATION OF LAW; PLAINTIFF'S EXCLUSIVE REMEDY IS IN WORKER'S COMPENSATION." THE REMAINING LANGUAGE IN THE 1OTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS STRICKEN. =(302/CWW/PL-X)

rulings.Case Title: MARY FIGONE et al VS. MICHAEL HALOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 22, 2009, LINE 9, CROSS-DEFENDANTS MARY FIGONE AND NANCY STEWART'S Motion For Attorney's Fees. CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE THE PREVAILING PARTIES ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO DISMISS THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS BASED ON CROSS-DEFENDANTS' PROTECTED FREE SPEECH OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON HIS CLAIMS. THE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL OF AN ALLEGED SLAPP SUIT WHILE A SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS PENDING CREATES A PRESUMPTION THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARE PREVAILING PARTIES. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO SHOW HE HAD DISMISSED BECAUSE HE HAD SUBSTANTIALLY ACHIEVED HIS GOAL THROUGH SETTLEMENT OR OTHER MEANS, OR FOR OTHER REASONS UNRELATED TO THE PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. (SEE COLTRAIN V. SHEWALTER (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 94, 107, WHERE MONETARY LOSS WAS INSUFFICIENT TO DISPEL PRESUMPTION.) ATTORNEY FEES GRANTED. RATES REASONABLE AND TIME EXPENDED REASONABLE AND NECESSARY. ATTORNEY FEES AWARDED IN LODESTAR AMOUNT OF $15,640 PLUS $80 COSTS. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA IRONWORKERS FIELD PENSION TRUST et al VS. 195 WILLOW STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 22, 2009, LINE 10, DEFENDANTS 195 WILLOW STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC, 818 VAN NESS, LLC, 81-816-826 VAN NESS LP DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. COMPLAINT UNCERTAIN IN THAT IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER PLAINTIFFS SEEK TO RECOVER UNDER SPECIFIC CONTRACTS, AND WHETHER THE CONTRACTS ARE ORAL, WRITTEN, OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AS TO WHO BREACHED WHICH CONTRACT, HOW NJR STEEL CAME TO BE INDEBTED TO PLAINTIFFS, AND WHO OWES DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS. COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN WHETHER PLAINTIFFS ASSERT A SIMPLE FORECLOSURE CLAIM AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS, OR AN ADDITIONAL BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM AGAINST INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. A FACTUAL ISSUE EXISTS WHETHER THE CLAIM IS BARRED AS UNTIMELY. THIS FACTUAL ISSUE WILL NOT BE RESOLVED BY WAY OF DEMURRER. =(302/CWW/JW)
